methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $2.4B.

data.narrative:
Beacon AI is a Series A-stage AI & Software company founded by Matt Cox, Avinash Nair, and Andrew Musto that has raised $15M+. The peer universe for valuation consists of 9 comparable companies: 8 private firms including Liquid AI, Field AI, and Twelve Labs, plus 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $15M raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $30M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Liquid AI at $2.4B, Field AI at $2.0B, and Twelve Labs at $250M. The median across these three peers is $2.0B, with a range spanning $250M to $2.4B.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $26M to $2.3B, with a median of $1.0B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate multiples and peer selection within frontier AI. The stage-progression method anchors a conservative floor, while peer comparables suggest substantially higher valuations conditional on Beacon's competitive positioning relative to Liquid AI and Field AI. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, capital efficiency, and whether Beacon tracks nearer to high-growth peers or the lower-quartile Twelve Labs comparable.
